reason: Both answers correctly recommend Postgres, cite SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, transactional enqueue as the key win, realistic throughput math, mature tooling options, and sensible switch triggers. Neither contains factual errors. Differences are minor: A lists a couple more language bindings and a shared-instance caveat; B gives slightly more explicit contrast on when SQS/Redis might still make sense and notes migration ease. These are marginal, non-decisive additions on both sides. Overall quality and correctness are equivalent.
